steps.title: Read the leg lengths
steps.content: The diagram shows a right triangle with leg lengths $3$ and $7$.

steps.title: Apply the theorem
steps.content:
Use the Pythagorean theorem with hypotenuse $c$:
$$c^2=3^2+7^2=9+49=58$$
$$c=\sqrt{58}$$

steps.title: Estimate the hypotenuse
steps.content:
Since $7^2=49$ and $8^2=64$, $\sqrt{58}$ is between $7$ and $8$. More precisely,
$$7.6^2=57.76$$
so $7.6$ is the closest choice. The answer is C.
